text: How do you typically create passwords for your online accounts?

options.label: I use the same simple password everywhere
I use a few variations of the same password
I create unique passwords but keep them short
I use long, unique passwords with mixed characters
I use a password manager to generate and store unique passwords

text: How often do you update your passwords for important accounts?

options.label: Never — I set them once and forget
Only when forced by the service
Once a year or so
Every few months for critical accounts
Regularly, and I use 2FA on all important accounts

text: Do you use two-factor authentication (2FA) on your accounts?

options.label: I don't know what 2FA is
I've heard of it but never set it up
I use it on one or two accounts
I use it on most important accounts
I use it on every account that supports it

text: You receive an urgent email from your bank asking you to verify your account. What do you do?

options.label: Click the link immediately — it looks official
Click only if it looks like it's from my bank
Check the sender's email address before clicking
Go directly to the bank's website without clicking the link
Report the email as suspicious and verify through official channels

text: How do you handle links shared in social media messages from people you know?

options.label: I click on anything my friends share
I click if the message seems normal
I'm cautious if the link looks unusual
I verify with the person before clicking unknown links
I never click links in messages — I search for content independently

text: A website offers a free gift card if you enter your email and phone number. What do you do?

options.label: Enter my real information — free stuff is great
Enter my email but not phone number
Search online to see if the offer is legitimate
Recognize it as a likely scam and close the page
Report the page and warn others about it

text: What privacy settings do you use on your social media profiles?

options.label: Everything is public — I haven't checked settings
Mostly public with a few restrictions
I've reviewed settings but left most things open
I've carefully configured who can see my posts and info
Strict privacy — limited profile, approved followers only

text: How do you handle app permissions on your phone?

options.label: I always accept all permissions without reading
I skim permissions but accept anyway
I deny permissions that seem unnecessary
I carefully review and limit all permissions
I regularly audit app permissions and revoke unused ones

text: What personal information do you share when signing up for new apps or services?

options.label: Whatever they ask for — full name, phone, address
Most fields including optional ones
Only required fields
Minimum required info, sometimes with aliases
I use burner emails and share minimal data

text: How do you keep your devices and software updated?

options.label: I ignore update notifications
I update when I remember or when forced
I update my phone but not other devices
I regularly update all devices and apps
Auto-updates enabled everywhere, and I verify critical patches

text: What do you do when connecting to public Wi-Fi?

options.label: Connect to any available network
Use public Wi-Fi for everything including banking
Avoid sensitive activities on public Wi-Fi
Use a VPN when on public networks
Avoid public Wi-Fi entirely or use VPN + verify network authenticity

text: How do you secure your phone and laptop?

options.label: No lock screen or password
Simple PIN like 1234
A reasonable password or pattern
Biometric lock plus strong password
Biometric + encryption + remote wipe capability enabled
